The application is a community social networking platform.
Admins keep the platform organized through basic moderation tools.
Users can register using an email address and a password.
Email verification is required before login is allowed.
Users can build a profile.
A profile includes a profile picture.
A profile includes a cover photo.
A profile includes a bio.
A profile includes a location.
Users can create text posts.
Users can optionally upload a single image to a post.
Users can optionally upload multiple images to a post.
Users can edit their own posts.
Users can delete their own posts.
Users can like a post.
Users can unlike a post.
Users can see a like count.
Users can comment on posts.
Comments are flat and non-nested.
Users can edit their own comments.
Users can delete their own comments.
There is a news feed.
The news feed lists posts from friends.
The news feed lists posts from followed users.
The news feed is sorted by most recent first.
Users can send friend requests.
Users can accept friend requests.
Users can reject friend requests.
There is a friends list page.
Users can follow other users.
Users can unfollow other users.
Following does not require mutual approval.
There is a followers count.
There is a following count.
Users can create groups.
A group has a name.
A group has a description.
A group has a public/private flag.
Users can join public groups directly.
Users can leave public groups directly.
Group creators can approve join requests for private groups.
Group creators can reject join requests for private groups.
There is a dedicated group feed.
The group feed lists only posts made within that group.
There is a basic direct messaging feature.
Direct messaging supports 1:1 text conversations.
Conversations are stored.
Conversations are retrieved like a simple chat log.
There are no real-time typing indicators.
There are no read receipts.
There is a notifications list.
Notifications are generated on action.
Notifications can be marked read.
Notifications can be marked unread.
Notifications are marked read or unread on click.
Notifications include likes.
Notifications include comments.
Notifications include friend requests.
There is basic keyword search.
Search covers users.
Search covers posts.
Search covers groups.
Search uses simple text matching.
No full-text engine is required.
Users can bookmark posts.
Users can save posts.
There is a single default 'Saved Posts' list.
There are no custom collections.
There is a 'Report Post' button.
Clicking the 'Report Post' button creates a report record.
The report record includes a reason dropdown.
The report is viewable in an admin list.
There is a simple admin panel.
Admins can view reported content.
Admins can delete a post.
Admins can suspend a user account.
Suspension uses a boolean active/suspended flag.
Users can set basic privacy on their profile.
Privacy options are public or private.
Privacy controls whether non-friends can view their posts.
Users can block another user.
Blocking hides the blocked user's posts from the blocker.
Blocking hides the blocker's posts from the blocked user.
Blocking prevents messaging between the two users.
Users can update notification preferences.
Notification preferences are per category.
Categories include likes, comments, and friend requests.
Preferences are on/off toggles.
Admins can grant a verified badge.
Admins can revoke a verified badge.
The verified badge is a boolean flag toggle.
Users can view their own post history.
Users can edit account settings.
Account settings include email.
Account settings include password.
Account settings include username.
There is basic pagination.
There is infinite scroll.
Pagination or infinite scroll applies to feeds.
Pagination or infinite scroll applies to comments.
Pagination or infinite scroll applies to search results.
Pagination uses limit/offset queries.
Users can delete their own account.
Account deletion is a soft delete.
Soft delete sets a deleted flag.
There is no hard deletion.
There are two roles: Admin and User.
Route guards are simple.
There is no granular permission matrix.
Admin actions are logged.
Logged admin actions include ban, delete, and verify.
Admin actions are logged to a basic activity log table.
This online social network allows individuals to create profiles and interact with others in a shared digital space.
The platform facilitates sharing of text and media content among its users and provides tools for community management.
Users can build personal profiles, post updates, and engage with friends and groups through likes and comments.
Using the service, members can build social connections, discover content, and participate in group discussions.
A community-based social application enables people to connect, share, and communicate within a moderated environment.
